mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS > 4.0% over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE < 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or Rank IC drops below 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or the signal reverses direction during inflationary supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 205.0, persistent inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of observations where CPIAUCSL > 205.0, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate indicate a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when PAYEMS growth falls below 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) over a 5-year rolling window, or returns are negative when GDP growth > 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict returns when CPIAUCSL is stable.
